carrier_arguments: Type of loss is Hail, date of loss 5/22/2024.
Aluminum coating on metal roofs at 139 Abbe Rd assessed at 'Below Avg' condition with 90% depreciation (maximum allowable), age 5 of 7 year life.
Loc 2-1 (139 Abbe Rd): RCV $4,672.80, ACV $467.28, deductible $3,730.00 — Net Claim $0.00. Total recoverable depreciation $942.80 if depreciation is recovered.
Loc 3-1 (120-134 Eady Ct): RCV $1,013.74, deductible $9,020.00 — Net Claim $0.00. Entire RCV absorbed by deductible.
Loc 1-1 (1540 Lowell St): RCV $333.33, deductible $49,450.00 — Net Claim $0.00. Entire RCV absorbed by deductible.
All three locations result in $0.00 net claim due to deductibles exceeding damage amounts.
Federated will not be responsible for any services, costs or expenses in excess of this scope and estimate without pre-approval of Marc Dellario.

enclosed_documents: Forensic Causation Report with annotated photography
Supplement report with line-by-line variance analysis
summary_paragraphs: Please find enclosed the complete forensic documentation package for the property at 139 Abbe Rd S, Elyria, OH 44035. Our independent inspection has documented storm-related damage to the Roofing.

The enclosed reports include annotated photography of all identified damage, an Xactimate-format replacement cost estimate, and a forensic causation analysis establishing the relationship between documented weather events and observed damage.
Please do not hesitate to contact us should you require any additional information or wish to schedule a joint inspection.

code_violations.requirement: Asphalt shingles shall be installed in accordance with this section AND the manufacturer's installation instructions — adopts manufacturer spec as code-enforceable requirement
code_violations.status: Required — manufacturer spec controls fastener count, exposure, starter, drip edge, and warranty-critical details

code_violations.requirement: Drip edge required at eaves and rake edges of shingle roofs; min 2-inch overlap at joints, min 1/4-inch projection below sheathing
Underlayment shall be applied in accordance with manufacturer's installation instructions — ASTM D226 / D4869 / D6757 labeling required
Ice barrier required in areas where the average daily temperature in January is ≤25°F or where ice damming has a history; min 2 feet inside exterior wall line (Climate Zones 5A+ including OH/NY)
Flashings shall be installed at wall and roof intersections, wherever there is a change in roof slope or direction, and around roof openings

conclusion_paragraphs: Based on our forensic analysis, the property at 139 Abbe Rd S, Elyria, OH 44035 sustained confirmed storm damage consistent with the May 22, 2024 weather event. The modified bitumen flat roof system exhibits damage patterns consistent with storm-related impact forces. The documented conditions, when evaluated collectively, establish a clear causal link between the reported storm event and the current state of the roofing system and associated components.

The confirmed damage to the modified bitumen flat roof system and associated components requires full system replacement rather than localized repair. Modified bitumen membranes, once compromised by impact damage, lose their waterproofing integrity across the affected field area, and partial repairs cannot restore the system to its pre-loss functional condition. Additionally, replacement must conform to current building code requirements under the RCO (and underlying IRC standards), which may trigger upgraded specifications for underlayment, fastening schedules, and material compliance that differ from the original installation — further necessitating a comprehensive scope of work.

Based on the documented damage, applicable building codes under the RCO, and recognized industry standards, we recommend full replacement of the modified bitumen flat roof system, including all associated flashings, edge metal, and underlayment components. The carrier's current recognized scope of $6,019.87 does not adequately address the full extent of documented storm damage, required code-compliant upgrades, or the material and labor costs associated with a proper system replacement. Our scope determination reflects the actual conditions observed and documented at the property, accounting for all necessary line items to restore the roof to its pre-loss condition in compliance with current codes.

This determination is supported by industry-accepted standards, including HAAG Engineering residential roof inspection protocols, NRCA guidelines for modified bitumen roof system evaluation and replacement, and applicable IRC provisions as adopted under the RCO. These standards collectively establish the thresholds for damage identification, non-repairability of compromised flat roof membranes, and the code-driven scope requirements that inform our professional recommendation.

executive_summary: On May 22, 2024, the commercial property located at 139 Abbe Rd S, Elyria, OH 44035 sustained storm damage to its modified bitumen / flat roof system and associated building components. A forensic inspection was conducted to document the condition of the roofing membrane, flashings, edge metal, and ancillary systems in order to establish the cause, scope, and extent of storm-related damage. This report presents the findings of that investigation and provides a technical basis for the recommended scope of repair.

The inspection identified storm-inflicted damage to the modified bitumen membrane consistent with impact forces and wind-driven stress. Observable indicators include puncturing, scuffing, and displacement of the membrane surface layer, along with deformation of soft-metal flashings and edge details that are characteristic of storm-event loading rather than long-term weathering. Gutter and drainage components exhibited mechanical deformation consistent with the same event. These damage signatures are distinguishable from normal wear patterns by their randomized distribution and acute fracture geometry.

The modified bitumen roof system at this property cannot be restored to pre-loss condition through spot repair. Partial patching of a membrane system compromises the monolithic waterproofing envelope and does not meet manufacturer warranty requirements or the performance standards referenced under the RCO and IRC Section R905.11 for modified bitumen installations. The aged membrane cannot be material-matched in color, granule profile, or polymer composition, and selective replacement introduces differential expansion joints that become future failure points. Full system replacement is the only technically sound remediation that restores code-compliant performance.

The carrier's approved scope of $6,019.87 does not adequately account for the full membrane replacement, code-required underlayment and flashing details, removal and disposal of the existing compromised system, or the edge-metal and drainage component repairs documented during the inspection. Our forensic analysis, grounded in the physical evidence at 139 Abbe Rd S, identifies a scope of necessary work that materially exceeds the carrier's current valuation. A detailed line-item estimate accompanies this report for the carrier's review.

noaa.episode_narrative: Multi-day storm window scan: [2024-05-20] At the surface, an occluded low moved north-northeastward from near western Lake Superior to northern ON, west of James Bay, during the late morning of the 22nd through the wee hours of the morning of the 23rd. Simultaneously, the trailing cold front advanced generally eastward from southeastern Lower MI to the western NY to northeast OH corridor, just east and southeast of Lake Erie. Clusters and lines of multicell thunderstorms developed along and ahead of the cold front. Severe hail and straight-line convective wind damage occurred in portions of northern OH during the late morning through late evening of the 22nd. In addition, training thunderstorms in Oberlin produced excessive rainfall during the evening of the 22nd. The storms occurred in a warm/moist sector comprised of primarily weak to moderate MUCAPE, moderate to strong DCAPE, and moderate to strong effective bulk shear. | [2024-05-21] At the surface, an occluded low moved north-northeastward from near western Lake Superior to northern ON, west of James Bay, during the late morning of the 22nd through the wee hours of the morning of the 23rd. Simultaneously, the trailing cold front advanced generally eastward from southeastern Lower MI to the western NY to northeast OH corridor, just east and southeast of Lake Erie. Clusters and lines of multicell thunderstorms developed along and ahead of the cold front. Severe hail and straight-line convective wind damage occurred in portions of northern OH during the late morning through late evening of the 22nd. In addition, training thunderstorms in Oberlin produced excessive rainfall during the evening of the 22nd. The storms occurred in a warm/moist sector comprised of primarily weak to moderate MUCAPE, moderate to strong DCAPE, and moderate to strong effective bulk shear.

weather.storm_description: Multi-day storm window scan: [2024-05-20] At the surface, an occluded low moved north-northeastward from near western Lake Superior to northern ON, west of James Bay, during the late morning of the 22nd through the wee hours of the morning of the 23rd. Simultaneously, the trailing cold front advanced generally eastward from southeastern Lower MI to the western NY to northeast OH corridor, just east and southeast of Lake Erie. Clusters and lines of multicell thunderstorms developed along and ahead of the cold front. Severe hail and straight-line convective wind damage occurred in portions of northern OH during the late morning through late evening of the 22nd. In addition, training thunderstorms in Oberlin produced excessive rainfall during the evening of the 22nd. The storms occurred in a warm/moist sector comprised of primarily weak to moderate MUCAPE, moderate to strong DCAPE, and moderate to strong effective bulk shear. | [2024-05-21] At the surface, an occluded low moved north-northeastward from near western Lake Superior to northern ON, west of James Bay, during the late morning of the 22nd through the wee hours of the morning of the 23rd.
